data.content:
Now I have a full picture. Here's the analysis of **all legacy patterns** found:

| # | Legacy Pattern | Modernization |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` | → `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Removed — SDK handles implicitly |
| 3 | `Configuration`/`Platform` conditional defaults | Removed — SDK defaults |
| 4 | `ProjectGuid` | Removed — not needed |
| 5 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 6 | `FileAlignment`, `ErrorReport`, `WarningLevel` | Removed — default values |
| 7 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Removed — default for exe; unnecessary for library |
| 8 | `Deterministic` | Removed — default true in SDK-style |
| 9 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | Removed — all values match SDK defaults |
| 10 | Explicit `<Reference>` for System assemblies | Removed — implicit for .NET Framework |
| 11 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | Removed — SDK uses file globbing |
| 12 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Removed — metadata moves to `<PropertyGroup>` |

Let me apply the modernization now.

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Non-SDK `<Project>` element** — uses `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and the XML namespace instead of `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports** — SDK-style handles these automatically
3. **Explicit `Configuration`/`Platform` defaults** — SDK provides these by default
4. **`ProjectGuid`** — not needed in SDK-style


5. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` (v4.7.2)** — replaced by `TargetFramework` (net472)
6. **`FileAlignment`** — default value, removable
7. **`AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`** — default true for exe in SDK-style, but for library it's not needed
8. **Debug/Release conditional PropertyGroups** — all values are defaults in SDK-style and can be removed
9. **Explicit `<Reference>` for framework assemblies** — implicit in SDK-style projects targeting .NET Framework
10. **Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items** — SDK-style automatically includes all source files by default
11. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — assembly metadata gets auto-generated in SDK-style, so I can move those attributes directly into the project file properties
12. **`Deterministic`** — already defaults to true in SDK-style

Now I'm ready to start the migration.

data.content:
**Done.** The project went from **47 lines → 14 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

- **Project root** → SDK-style `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`
- **Target framework** → `net472` (new short form)
- **Removed entirely**: `Import` statements, `ProjectGuid`, `Configuration`/`Platform` defaults, Debug/Release property groups, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, all `<Reference>` items, all `<Compile>` items (SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs`)
- **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** → deleted; metadata preserved as `<Description>`, `<Company>`, `<Product>`, `<Copyright>`, and `<Version>` in the project file (SDK auto-generates assembly attributes from these)
- **`OutputType`** omitted since `Library` is the SDK default
